danger_signals.visual_form: player sprite arrangement and color differentiation on field
danger_signals.meaning: tactical threat; indicates opponent positioning and play direction

danger_signals.visual_form: sprites moving toward ball carrier with collision detection
danger_signals.meaning: immediate physical danger to player with ball; tackle imminent

danger_signals.visual_form: rain or snow visual overlay on playing field
danger_signals.meaning: reduced traction and visibility; affects gameplay difficulty and ball control

safety_signals.visual_form: large unoccupied grid areas on screen between ball carrier and defenders
safety_signals.meaning: running room available; low immediate threat

safety_signals.visual_form: own team sprites clustered near ball position
safety_signals.meaning: offensive line protection; safe pocket for quarterback operations

safety_signals.visual_form: sunny visual presentation without precipitation overlay
safety_signals.meaning: optimal playing conditions; standard ball physics apply

faction_indicators.color_identity: primary team color palette (varies by NFL team: Cowboys blue, 49ers red, etc.)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: official NFL team logo displayed in end zones (16-bit versions)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Opposing team rendered in contrasting color scheme; distinct jersey and helmet colors
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: team colors dominate scoreboard and UI elements; home field advantage implicit in presentation

faction_indicators.color_identity: secondary/alternate team color palette (away jersey colors)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: opposing NFL team logo in opposite end zone
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: sprite palette swaps distinguish from home team; uniform color differentiation
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: secondary color scheme in opposing score display; visual separation from home team presentation

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: standard field layout with team logos, scoreboard displaying quarter and time, stat tracking for player performance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: standard AI difficulty; full playbook options available; statistical records accumulate across game

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: enhanced visual presentation; all-star roster composition reflected in player sprite selection
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: accelerated quarter options (15-min, 10-min); modified AI to reflect elite player skill levels

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: rosters update year-to-year (16-bit versions support 1991-1993 seasons); player attributes visually reflected in performance metrics
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: no rookies from next draft cycle appear; player team changes persist; statistical achievements accumulate seasonally

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: rain or snow overlay on field; reduced visibility; wet surface appearance
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: ball physics altered; player movement slowed; pass accuracy reduced; random weather occurrence in season mode

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scoreboard clearly displays point gap between teams
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: CPU AI adjusts playcalling based on game state (implied); player behavioral urgency tied to visual score display

environmental_storytelling.example: official NFL team logos in end zones (16-bit versions) establish geographic identity and league legitimacy; real team branding communicates professional context
real NFL players with accurate attributes (Bo Jackson carrying ball) tell story of actual 1990-91 season; missing NFLPA non-members (Jim Kelly as generic QB) signal licensing boundaries
quarter display, time remaining, score, and statistical tracking create temporal and competitive narrative; visible progression from game start to conclusion
three weather conditions (sunny, rain, snow) establish stadium location and seasonal context; impacts gameplay difficulty and visual atmosphere
consistent NFL field markings and dimensions communicate sport authenticity; yard line markers and end zone boundaries establish spatial reference

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: sprite movement becomes erratic when defensive player proximity triggers; evasion animations activate; ball vulnerability increases to interception
offensive line sprite positioning creates visual buffer; longer play execution window; pass completion probability higher
defensive player penetration visible through line gaps; rapid sack potential; sprint animations activate for escape attempts
coordinated sprite arrangement across field; gap coverage displayed through positioning; play read communication implicit in formation structure
defensive back sprite proximity to receiver; visual occlusion or tight marking; interception risk increases
separation distance from defenders; clear line of sight to ball; catch probability enhanced
visual indicators of ball carrier vulnerability; defensive clustering around ball position; flash or pulse animation on contested ball

notes: Tecmo Super Bowl communicates game state primarily through team color differentiation, field positioning of sprites, scoreboard data, and environmental overlays (weather). The game employs minimal HUD abstraction, instead embedding tactical information in player formation visibility and spatial relationships. The 16-bit versions enhance this communication through official NFL logos and improved visual fidelity. Political/faction dimensions are implicit in real team/player licensing rather than explicit antagonism narratives. The paradigm note 'racing' in source metadata appears erroneous; game is sports/football simulation.
